subject: Statistics
title: Moore's Law: The Self-Fulfilling Forecast
description: A short lesson to help you apply Moore's Law: The Self-Fulfilling Forecast.

hook.text: In 1965, Gordon Moore predicted transistor counts would double every two years. It held for over 50 years.

buildup.text: Moore's Law isn't a physical law — it's an observation about the pace of engineering progress.

discovery.text: The semiconductor industry used Moore's Law as a roadmap, setting targets to match the prediction.

twist.text: A forecast that guides investment becomes self-fulfilling. The prediction created its own success.

climax.text: Moore's Law is slowing as transistors approach atomic limits. The self-fulfilling loop is finally hitting physics.

punchline.text: The best forecast in history worked because everyone believed it.

quiz.question: Why did Moore's Law hold for over 50 years?
options: The industry used it as a roadmap, setting targets that became self-fulfilling
It's a fundamental law of physics
Transistors naturally double at that rate without human effort
